title: Telemetry Backend — Datadog
description: Datadog all-in-one managed observability platform. Provides metrics, structured logging, distributed tracing (APM), and dashboards as a unified SaaS backend with no infrastructure to operate.

cost.costNotes: Consumption-based: $15-23/host/month infrastructure + $0.10/GB logs + $2.30/host/month APM. Small (5 hosts, 5GB logs/day): ~$90/month. Medium (20 hosts, APM, 20GB logs/day): ~$600/month. Costs escalate quickly with high log volume or many hosts.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Datadog pricing tiers (2025): Infrastructure Pro $23/host/month, Log Management $0.10/GB ingested ($1.70/million log events), APM $2.30/host/month. Min ($90): 5 hosts infra + 5GB/day logs (~150GB/month). Max ($3000): 20 hosts with APM + high log volume (500GB/month) + custom metrics.
provenance.adoptionCost: Low adoption cost due to managed SaaS: agent installation (~4h), dashboard/alert setup (~16h), team onboarding (~4h). Total ~24h at $40/hour = ~$960.
provenance.licenseCost: No upfront license fee; all costs are consumption-based subscription (included in monthly estimate).

provides.reasoning: Datadog is a fully managed observability backend — it receives metrics, logs, and traces from agents and the OpenTelemetry Collector, stores them, and provides query and alerting interfaces.
Datadog Log Management ingests, parses, and indexes structured logs. Supports JSON log ingestion with automatic field extraction and correlation with traces via trace_id injection.
Datadog Metrics stores all ingested metrics as time-series data with 15-month retention at 1-second granularity, queryable via PromQL-compatible APIs.
Datadog APM provides end-to-end distributed tracing with automatic instrumentation, flame graphs, and service maps. Supports OpenTelemetry export via the Datadog OTLP endpoint.
Datadog captures and tracks discrete application events through custom metrics, log events, and APM events, providing event-tracking infrastructure for compliance, alerting, and business analytics.

availability.reason: Commercially available SaaS; no access restrictions

supports_nfr.reason: Telemetry backend is meaningful from 90% availability upward; below that, service reliability is the primary concern, not observability infrastructure.
Datadog's own SLA is 99.9%; at 4-nines availability targets a secondary alerting path is required, making this pattern insufficient as a sole observability backend.
Datadog supports audit trail features compatible with or without formal audit logging requirements.
Datadog is a managed SaaS with 99.9% SLA; agent reconnection after service restart takes <1 minute. RTO >= 5 minutes is comfortably supported.
Datadog scales to millions of metrics per hour as a managed SaaS; throughput is not a limiting factor.
Datadog agent collects metrics asynchronously on a push model; it does not add per-request overhead to application read paths.
Datadog APM uses asynchronous trace export with local buffering; it does not constrain application write throughput.
Datadog is GDPR compliant and offers EU data residency options; compatible with or without GDPR requirements.
Datadog offers a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) for HIPAA-regulated customers on enterprise plans.
Datadog is CCPA compliant and provides data subject access controls; compatible with CCPA requirements.
Datadog audit logs and access controls support SOX compliance requirements; compatible with or without SOX.

supports_constraints.reason: Datadog is cloud-agnostic and supports agents on any cloud, on-premises, or hybrid environment.
Datadog variant is only selected when the user has explicitly approved Datadog as an acceptable SaaS provider.
Datadog provides agents and APM libraries for all major programming languages.
Datadog supports monitoring across all platform types.

metrics_retention_days.description: Datadog metrics retention period. 15 days on default plan; longer retention requires higher plan tier.

log_retention_days.description: Log retention in Datadog. Shorter retention reduces log storage costs significantly.

apm_enabled.description: Enable Datadog APM (distributed tracing). Adds $2.30/host/month but provides full distributed tracing and flame graphs.

warn_nfr.reason: Datadog's SLA is 99.9%; for 4-nines availability, a secondary observability path or multi-vendor strategy is needed.
warn_nfr.message: obs-telemetry-backend--datadog: availability target {actual} (≥99.99%) exceeds Datadog's own 99.9% SLA. Consider a secondary alerting path for observability continuity.
